column3: ōrātiōnem meam quum dēprecor~; ā timōre inimīcī ēripe animam meam. ^ Hear
column5: my prayer when I make supplication to thee~; deliver my soul from the fear of the enemy.

column2: ā multitūdine operantium inīquitātem. ^ Thou hast protected me from the assembly of the malignant~; from the multitude of the workers of iniquity.

column1: 4 ^ Quia exacuērunt ut gladium linguās suās~; intendērunt arcum rem amāram
column2: ^ For they have whetted their tongues like a sword~; they have bent their bow a bitter thing

column1: 5 ^ ut sagittent in occultīs immaculātum. ^ to shoot in secret the undefiled.

column2: et nōn timēbunt~; firmāvērunt sibī sermōnem nēquam. Narrāvērunt ut absconderent laqueōs~; dixērunt
column3: «~¿~Quis vidēbit eōs~?~». ^ They will shoot at him on a sudden
column4: and will not fear~; they are resolute in wickedness. They have talked of hiding snares~; they have said

column1: 7 ^ Scrūtātī sunt inīquitātēs~; dēfēcērunt scrūtantēs scrūtiniō. Accēdet homō ad cor altum
column2: ^ They have searched after iniquities~; they have failed in their search. Man shall come to a deep heart

column1: 8 ^ et exaltābitur Deus. Sagittae parvulōrum factae sunt plāgae eōrum
column2: ^ and God shall be exalted. The arrows of children are their wounds

column1: 9 ^ et infirmātae sunt contrā eōs linguae eōrum. Conturbātī sunt omnēs quī vidēbant eōs
column2: ^ and their tongues against them are made weak. All that saw them were troubled

column1: 10 ^ et timuit omnis homō. Et adnūntiāvērunt opera Deī
column2: et facta ejus intellēxērunt. ^ and every man was afraid. And they declared the works of God

column3: et laudābuntur omnēs rectī corde. ^ The just shall rejoice in the Lord
column5: and all the upright in heart shall be praised.
